So they weren't running straight line patterns. The point was he had gone to this point. They disagreed on the fundamentals of the system was riskless, riskless or had risk. Because what you'll see happens as soon as I make him more certain, he suddenly finds the money. Liquidity is a relative term. I'm not liquid. I can't afford it means that maybe I'm not as liquid as I'd like to be right now. But when someone becomes really, really fucking certain, you know what? But I'll stretch and buy it anyway, because I know that's how great this is going to help me. They'll stretch and buy it anyway. So don't let, I'm not liquid. Oh, I love, I don't care about that one bit. Okay. 98% of the time it's just not true. Not that they're lying to you, but what they're saying is I'm not as liquid as I'd like to be. And when you make them more certain, the liquidity drops away. The point is though, is that my totality is slightly different with that objection. It's like, you know, I, I, I understand that, John. Totally get that. Let me ask you a question. Just the idea. Get it. And with that, well, and the formal part of today's show, which was a really important thing. Remember your anchors, guys, and let us start the Q&A. Awesome. Awesome. Great. Great training. Thank you. The first one's from Jorge. He says, what do you do once you close, what do you do once you close to make sure you don't create a buyer's remorse, create buyers remorse and keep tight rapport? Great question. So number one, couple things. Buyer's remorse, the main source of buyer's remorse is when someone buys something and you haven't really made that full scream from the hilltops air type logical case. And you had someone that would be had a sort of a lower action threshold. See, people with high action thresholds don't get buyers remorse. You know why? Cause they don't fucking buy unless they're damn certain. Seriously, it's more people like me who get buyers remorse. You just say, I'm easy to sell to them. I say, what did I just do? I don't really need this thing. I don't really want to say kind of piece of shit anyway. I don't know what I, is it really worth it? Did I really do the right thing? That's buyer's remorse. So what, what it really means is that the person doesn't really understand logically why they made the decision. So they feel bad about it. They were emotionally impulse. So the way you overcome the way you combat that one way for sure is to make sure that you make an air type logical case and you reinforce it as you loop. That's number one. In terms of other things you could do, believe it or not, ready? The best way, ask for referrals. If you get someone to give you a referral on the spot, the best time to ask referrals is right there on the spot in the first call. Once someone gives you a referral, they're not going to have buyers remorse. So now they've already told their friends, how can they, they even start giving you names. Right. Then how could they change their mind? They're almost becoming consistent with their actions. That's another way. Then there's all these other strategies, which are called stick strategies. And there's all this, there's a whole different topic. It's a training into itself here, but there are all these different ways where follow up letters, how your product is packaged. If you're a physical product, perfect example is Apple. Right. Have you ever bought something from Apple, like an iPhone? When you open up that box, it's like, whoa, fucking hell, fucking. It was care of this gleaming and shiny and like, whoa, it's beautiful. Right. Versus from a different company, open the box cracks in the middle. And the thing is, you know, like what the fuck?
